title: Ancient Shark Breaks Age Record
story: Scientists made a jaw-dropping discovery in 2016. A Greenland shark swam into the record books as the longest-living vertebrate on Earth! This incredible creature was found to be 392 years old. That means it was swimming in the icy waters long before the United States even existed. title: Tiny Moon Spotted Near Mars
story: Peering through a powerful telescope, astronomer Asaph Hall made an exciting discovery in 1877. He spotted a tiny moon orbiting Mars! This moon, later named Deimos, was so small and faint that no one had seen it before. Hall's sharp eyes and patience paid off on August 11, revealing one of Mars's hidden secrets. Deimos turned out to be a lumpy, potato-shaped rock, much smaller than Earth's moon.
activity_question: What did Asaph Hall discover in 1877?
activity_choices: A giant crater on the Moon
bonus_fact: Deimos means 'panic' in Greek, named after one of the mythical horses that pulled the war god Mars's chariot.

title: Cricket's First Double-Century Stand
story: Bats were swinging and records were falling in London! On August 11, 1884, Australian cricketers Percy McDonnell and Billy Murdoch made history. They became the first pair to score a double-century stand in Test cricket. McDonnell hit 103 runs while Murdoch smashed an impressive 211. Their incredible teamwork helped Australia draw the third Test match against England.
activity_question: What cricket record did McDonnell and Murdoch set?
activity_choices: First double-century stand in Test cricket
Highest individual score in a Test match
Most sixes hit in an innings
activity_answer: First double-century stand in Test cricket
bonus_fact: A century in cricket means scoring 100 runs, so a double-century is an amazing 200 runs!

title: Chad Breaks Free from French Rule
story: Freedom rang out across Chad as the country declared its independence from France in 1960. After years of being controlled by others, the people of Chad finally got to make their own choices. On August 11, they celebrated their new beginning as an independent nation in Africa. It was a day of joy, hope, and pride for Chadians.
activity_question: What did Chad declare in 1960?
follow_up_question: Can you name another African country that gained independence from European rule?

title: Folk Stars Surprise TV Viewers
story: Excitement filled the air as the Kingston Trio made a surprise appearance on the popular game show 'What's My Line?' in 1963. The folk music group, known for their catchy tunes, kept the panelists guessing on August 11. Viewers at home were in on the secret, eagerly watching as the trio tried to stump the show's celebrity guests.
activity_question: What popular game show did the Kingston Trio appear on?
bonus_fact: The Kingston Trio helped spark the folk music revival of the late 1950s and early 1960s.

title: Cessna's Big Bird Takes Flight
story: Zoom! On August 11, 1956, a giant metal bird spread its wings. The Cessna 620, a huge airplane with four powerful engines, took its first flight. This massive machine was Cessna's biggest plane yet. It soared through the sky, showing off its strength and size. Though it never became popular, the 620's flight was an exciting moment in aviation history.
activity_question: What kind of aircraft took its first flight in this story?
bonus_fact: Cessna is famous for making small planes, not just big ones!

title: Silver Strikes in the Wild West
story: Miners struck it rich in Virginia City, Nevada! On August 11, 1860, the first successful silver mill in the United States began crushing ore. This sparked a silver rush, drawing fortune seekers from far and wide. The bustling boomtown grew rapidly as more and more silver was discovered in the nearby Comstock Lode.
activity_question: Where was the first successful silver mill in the United States located?
bonus_fact: Virginia City's population exploded from just a few hundred to about 25,000 people in just a few years.

title: A Star-Studded Career Begins
story: Gazing at the night sky, George B. Airy embarked on an incredible journey in 1835. On August 11, he stepped into the role of England's Astronomer Royal. Little did anyone know, this starry-eyed scientist would guide British astronomy for nearly half a century! Airy's sharp mind and dedication would shape how we study the cosmos for generations to come.
activity_question: What position did George B. Airy take on August 11, 1835?
follow_up_question: Why do you think the role of Astronomer Royal was important?
